questions.text: Heron's formula for area of a triangle with sides a, b, c is:
questions.explanation: Area = sqrt(s(s-a)(s-b)(s-c)) where s is the semi-perimeter.

questions.text: In Heron's formula, 's' represents:
questions.explanation: s = (a + b + c) / 2, the semi-perimeter (half of perimeter).

questions.text: The semi-perimeter of a triangle with sides 3 cm, 4 cm and 5 cm is:
The area of an equilateral triangle with side 'a' is:
questions.explanation: Area of equilateral triangle = (sqrt(3)/4) x a^2.

questions.text: Heron's formula is most useful when:

options.a: all three sides are known but height is not
options.c: two sides and angle are known

questions.explanation: Heron's formula allows us to find area using only the three sides, without needing the height.

questions.text: The area of triangle with sides 3 cm, 4 cm, 5 cm using Heron's formula is:
questions.explanation: s = 6. Area = sqrt(6 x 3 x 2 x 1) = sqrt(36) = 6 sq cm.

questions.text: The perimeter of a triangle with sides 5 cm, 12 cm, 13 cm is:
questions.explanation: Perimeter = 5 + 12 + 13 = 30 cm.

questions.text: For Heron's formula to give a valid positive area, which condition must hold?

options.a: s > a, s > b and s > c

questions.explanation: Each factor (s-a), (s-b) and (s-c) must be positive, so s must be greater than each side.

questions.text: The area of an equilateral triangle with side 4 cm is:
questions.explanation: Area = (sqrt(3)/4) x 4^2 = (sqrt(3)/4) x 16 = 4 sqrt(3) sq cm.

questions.text: If the semi-perimeter of a triangle is 12 cm, then its perimeter is:
questions.explanation: Perimeter = 2 x semi-perimeter = 2 x 12 = 24 cm.

questions.text: A triangle with sides 5 cm, 12 cm and 13 cm is a:
questions.explanation: 5^2 + 12^2 = 25 + 144 = 169 = 13^2. By converse of Pythagorean theorem, it is right-angled.

questions.text: Area of a right triangle with legs 5 cm and 12 cm is:
questions.explanation: Area = (1/2) x base x height = (1/2) x 5 x 12 = 30 sq cm.

questions.text: To find the area of a quadrilateral using Heron's formula, we:
questions.explanation: A diagonal divides the quadrilateral into two triangles. We find each area using Heron's formula and add.

questions.text: The area of an isosceles triangle with equal sides 5 cm and base 6 cm is:
questions.explanation: s = (5+5+6)/2 = 8. Area = sqrt(8 x 3 x 3 x 2) = sqrt(144) = 12 sq cm.

questions.text: Semi-perimeter of a triangle with perimeter 42 cm is:
questions.explanation: s = perimeter / 2 = 42 / 2 = 21 cm.

questions.text: The area of a triangle with sides 6 cm, 8 cm and 10 cm is:
questions.explanation: 6^2 + 8^2 = 100 = 10^2, so it is right-angled. Area = (1/2) x 6 x 8 = 24 sq cm.

questions.text: If area of equilateral triangle = 9 sqrt(3) sq cm, find the side:
questions.explanation: (sqrt(3)/4) x a^2 = 9 sqrt(3), so a^2 = 36, a = 6 cm.

questions.text: Heron's formula is named after:
questions.explanation: Heron (or Hero) of Alexandria was a Greek mathematician who developed this formula.

questions.text: For a triangle with sides 7 cm, 8 cm and 9 cm, the value of (s-a) where a=7 and s=12 is:
The area of an equilateral triangle with perimeter 24 cm is:
questions.explanation: Side = 24/3 = 8 cm. Area = (sqrt(3)/4) x 64 = 16 sqrt(3) sq cm.

questions.text: Find the area of triangle with sides 13 cm, 14 cm and 15 cm:
questions.explanation: s = 21. Area = sqrt(21 x 8 x 7 x 6) = sqrt(7056) = 84 sq cm.

questions.text: Find the area of equilateral triangle with side 6 cm:
questions.explanation: Area = (sqrt(3)/4) x 36 = 9 sqrt(3) sq cm.

questions.text: A triangular garden has sides 40 m, 24 m and 32 m. Find its area:
questions.explanation: s = 48. Area = sqrt(48 x 8 x 24 x 16) = sqrt(147456) = 384 sq m.

questions.text: An isosceles triangle has perimeter 32 cm and base 12 cm. Find its area:
questions.explanation: Equal sides = (32-12)/2 = 10 cm. s = 16. Area = sqrt(16 x 6 x 6 x 4) = sqrt(2304) = 48 sq cm.

questions.text: The sides of a triangle are in ratio 3:4:5 and perimeter is 48 cm. Find area:
questions.explanation: Sides: 12, 16, 20 cm. Right triangle (3:4:5 ratio). Area = (1/2) x 12 x 16 = 96 sq cm.

questions.text: An equilateral triangle has perimeter 36 cm. Find its area:
questions.explanation: Side = 36/3 = 12 cm. Area = (sqrt(3)/4) x 144 = 36 sqrt(3) sq cm.

questions.text: Find area of triangle with sides 9 cm, 40 cm and 41 cm:
A rhombus has diagonals 12 cm and 16 cm. Find its area:
questions.explanation: Area of rhombus = (1/2) x d1 x d2 = (1/2) x 12 x 16 = 96 sq cm.

questions.text: A triangle has perimeter 12 cm and sides in ratio 3:4:5. Find area using Heron's formula:
questions.explanation: Sides: 3, 4, 5 cm. s = 6. Area = sqrt(6 x 3 x 2 x 1) = sqrt(36) = 6 sq cm.

questions.text: Sides of a triangular plot are 10 m, 24 m and 26 m. Find area:
A quadrilateral ABCD has AB = 9 m, BC = 40 m, CD = 28 m, DA = 15 m and diagonal AC = 41 m. Find total area:
The area of equilateral triangle is 64 sqrt(3) sq cm. Find its perimeter:
questions.explanation: (sqrt(3)/4)a^2 = 64 sqrt(3), a^2 = 256, a = 16 cm. Perimeter = 3 x 16 = 48 cm.

questions.text: Sides of a triangle are in ratio 12:17:25 and perimeter is 540 m. Find area:
A rhombus with perimeter 40 cm has one diagonal 12 cm. Find its area:
questions.explanation: Side = 10 cm. Half diagonals: 6 and sqrt(100-36)=8. Full diagonals: 12 and 16. Area=(1/2)x12x16=96 sq cm.

questions.text: If area of equilateral triangle with side 'a' is A, find area of equilateral triangle with side '2a':
questions.explanation: Area with side 2a = (sqrt(3)/4)(2a)^2 = (sqrt(3)/4)(4a^2) = 4 x (sqrt(3)/4)a^2 = 4A.

questions.text: A right isosceles triangle has hypotenuse 10 cm. Find its area:
questions.explanation: Each leg = 10/sqrt(2) = 5 sqrt(2) cm. Area = (1/2) x 5 sqrt(2) x 5 sqrt(2) = (1/2) x 50 = 25 sq cm.

questions.text: Find the area of triangle with sides 3 cm, 4 cm and 5 cm using Heron's formula (in sq cm).
questions.explanation: s = 6. Area = sqrt(6 x 3 x 2 x 1) = sqrt(36) = 6 sq cm.

questions.text: Find the semi-perimeter of a triangle with sides 7 cm, 8 cm and 9 cm (in cm).
Area of equilateral triangle with side 8 cm = p sqrt(3) sq cm. Find p.
questions.explanation: Area = (sqrt(3)/4) x 64 = 16 sqrt(3) sq cm. So p = 16.

questions.text: Find the area of triangle with sides 13 cm, 14 cm and 15 cm (in sq cm).
questions.explanation: s = 21. Area = sqrt(21 x 8 x 7 x 6) = sqrt(7056) = 84 sq cm.

questions.text: A triangular board has sides 5 cm, 5 cm and 6 cm. Find its area (in sq cm).
questions.explanation: s = 8. Area = sqrt(8 x 3 x 3 x 2) = sqrt(144) = 12 sq cm.

questions.text: Find perimeter of equilateral triangle whose area is 25 sqrt(3) sq cm (in cm).
questions.explanation: (sqrt(3)/4)a^2 = 25 sqrt(3), a^2 = 100, a = 10 cm. Perimeter = 30 cm.

questions.text: Find area of triangle with sides 12 cm, 35 cm and 37 cm (in sq cm).
Find area of right triangle with hypotenuse 25 cm and one leg 7 cm (in sq cm).
questions.explanation: Other leg = sqrt(25^2 - 7^2) = sqrt(625-49) = sqrt(576) = 24 cm. Area = (1/2) x 7 x 24 = 84 sq cm.

questions.text: An isosceles triangle has equal sides 10 cm and base 12 cm. Find area (in sq cm).
Find the area of equilateral triangle with side 12 cm. Express as p sqrt(3) and find p.
questions.explanation: Area = (sqrt(3)/4) x 144 = 36 sqrt(3) sq cm. p = 36.

cases.case_text: A farmer owns a triangular field with sides 40 m, 24 m and 32 m. He wants to calculate the exact area of his field so he can buy the right amount of fertiliser. His neighbour owns a square field whose perimeter equals the perimeter of the triangular field. Both farmers compare their land areas for a local competition.

subparts.text: The semi-perimeter of the triangular field is:
The area of the triangular field is:
subparts.explanation: Area = sqrt(48 x 8 x 24 x 16) = sqrt(147456) = 384 sq m.

subparts.text: The perimeter of the triangular field equals the perimeter of the square field. Find the side of the square field (in metres).
subparts.explanation: Perimeter of triangle = 40+24+32 = 96 m. Side of square = 96/4 = 24 m.

cases.case_text: A school has a rhombus-shaped garden ABCD. Both diagonals are drawn, intersecting at point O. The diagonals have lengths AC = 16 m and BD = 12 m. The groundskeeper observes that the diagonals divide the garden into 4 congruent right triangles. Each right triangle has legs AO = 8 m and BO = 6 m. The school wants to plant grass in the garden and put a boundary fence around it.

subparts.text: Find the length of side AB of the rhombus using Pythagorean theorem (AO = 8 m, BO = 6 m):
subparts.explanation: AB = sqrt(AO^2 + BO^2) = sqrt(64 + 36) = sqrt(100) = 10 m.

subparts.text: The area of the rhombus garden is:
Find the perimeter of the rhombus garden (in metres).
